description.value: High-performance, column-oriented SQL database management system designed for real-time online analytical processing (OLAP) over billions of rows with millisecond latency

license.comment: Apache-2.0 (spdx_id Apache-2.0 reported by the GitHub API).

latest-version.comment: Latest stable release v26.4.4.38-stable (June 8, 2026) as of 2026-06-09; ClickHouse uses a CalVer-style YY.M versioning scheme.

database-type.comment: Column-oriented relational (SQL) OLAP DBMS. DB-Engines classifies it primarily as Relational DBMS; document/spatial/time-series/vector are secondary models. No dedicated 'columnar/OLAP' tag in the schema, so mapped to the closest 'relational' tag.

acid-compliant.comment: Not fully ACID by default: only single-block/single-partition MergeTree inserts are atomic/isolated/durable. Full multi-statement BEGIN/COMMIT transactions are experimental, require Keeper/ZooKeeper, and are unavailable in ClickHouse Cloud. Fails the 'full ACID by default' guideline.

schema-model.comment: Tables require typed column definitions (schema-enforced). A native dynamic JSON type exists for semi-structured data, but the table schema itself is enforced.

horizontal-scaling.comment: Native sharding via distributed tables and ReplicatedMergeTree distributes data across multiple nodes; scaling to billions of rows is a core design goal.

replication.comment: Replication is asynchronous and multi-master: INSERT/ALTER queries can be sent to any replica. Requires ClickHouse Keeper or ZooKeeper for coordination.

clustering.comment: Built-in clustering via shards, ReplicatedMergeTree, and distributed tables, coordinated by the bundled ClickHouse Keeper (or ZooKeeper); no third-party tooling required.

cloud-offering.comment: ClickHouse Cloud is the official managed service on AWS, GCP, and Azure.

full-text-search.comment: Text (inverted) indexes for full-text search became generally available in ClickHouse 26.2, supporting tokenizers across String, Array, Map, and JSON columns.

json-support.comment: Native, queryable JSON data type (production-ready since 25.3) with path-based querying, type inference, and sub-column extraction.

geospatial.comment: Native geo functions (distances on a sphere, geohashes, H3 indexes) and Point/Polygon geometric types.

time-series.comment: Dedicated (experimental) TimeSeries table engine for Prometheus remote-write/read protocols, plus general columnar suitability for time-series workloads. Engine is experimental (requires allow_experimental_time_series_table).

stored-procedures.comment: No traditional stored procedures with control flow; ClickHouse offers user-defined functions (UDFs) instead and recommends procedural logic in application code.

triggers.comment: No traditional CREATE TRIGGER support; (incremental) materialized views cover some event-driven use cases but are not database triggers.

views.comment: Supports normal, materialized, refreshable materialized, and window views.

official-gui.comment: The server ships a built-in web UI ('Play') at the HTTP interface (http://localhost:8123/play) for running queries; ClickHouse Cloud also provides an official web console.

docker-image.comment: An official image is published by ClickHouse itself (clickhouse/clickhouse-server), but it is not a Docker Official Image (not in the docker.io/_/ library namespace).

primary-language.comment: C++ is the primary language (~70% of the codebase).

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Could not fetch the Stack Overflow 'clickhouse' tag question count from a reliable source during this pass (stackoverflow.com is not fetchable in this environment).

db-engines-rank.comment: Ranked #26 overall on db-engines.com as of 2026-06-09.

learning-curve.comment: Standard SQL interface and strong documentation lower the entry barrier, but OLAP-specific concepts (MergeTree engine family, sharding/distributed tables, limited transactions, denormalized data modeling) add moderate complexity. Rated mid-range.
